carriageReturn: /\r\n|\r/g,
  spaceLine: /^ +$/gm,
  notSpaceStart: /^\S*/,
  endingNewline: /\n$/,
  listItemRegex: (bull: string) => new RegExp(`^( {0,3}${bull})((?:[\t ][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
  nextBulletRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:[*+-]|\\d{1,9}[.)])((?:[ \t][^\\n]*)?(?:\\n|$))`),
  hrRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}((?:- *){3,}|(?:_ *){3,}|(?:\\* *){3,})(?:\\n+|$)`),
  fencesBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}(?:\`\`\`|~~~)`),
  headingBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}#`),
  htmlBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}<(?:[a-z].*>|!--)`, 'i'),
};

If `this.rules.other.blockquoteBeginRegex` doesn't exist in the type definition, then `this.rules.other.blockquoteBeginRegex(indent)` would be a TypeScript error, and consequently `blockquoteBeginRegex` would never be "read" (never used successfully).

input.description: Test the three reported cases with source TypeScript

content.content:
=== Child (blockquote should be inside nested item) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

      quoteblock

=== Sibling (blockquote should be sibling of nested item, inside outer) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

    quoteblock

=== Parent (blockquote should be outside list) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

quote block

tool_use_result.stdout:
=== Child (blockquote should be inside nested item) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

      quoteblock

=== Sibling (blockquote should be sibling of nested item, inside outer) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

    quoteblock

=== Parent (blockquote should be outside list) ===
  • list item
    • nested list item

quote block

content.text:
All three cases now produce the correct output:

- **Child** (`    > quoteblock`, 4 spaces): blockquote is inside the nested list item ✓
- **Sibling** (`  > quoteblock`, 2 spaces): blockquote is a sibling of the nested item, inside the outer list item ✓  
- **Parent** (`> quote block`, 0 spaces): blockquote breaks out of the list entirely ✓

The fix was minimal — two files changed:

**`src/rules.ts`**: Added `blockquoteBeginRegex` alongside the other indent-aware regex factories:
```ts
blockquoteBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}>`),
```

**`src/Tokenizer.ts`**: Declared and applied the regex in the list item continuation loop, exactly mirroring how `fencesBeginRegex`, `headingBeginRegex`, and `htmlBeginRegex` already work. When the next line is a blockquote with fewer leading spaces than the current list item's `indent`, the loop breaks and stops consuming lines for that item — letting the blockquote be handled at the appropriate nesting level.

result:
All three cases now produce the correct output:

- **Child** (`    > quoteblock`, 4 spaces): blockquote is inside the nested list item ✓
- **Sibling** (`  > quoteblock`, 2 spaces): blockquote is a sibling of the nested item, inside the outer list item ✓  
- **Parent** (`> quote block`, 0 spaces): blockquote breaks out of the list entirely ✓

The fix was minimal — two files changed:

**`src/rules.ts`**: Added `blockquoteBeginRegex` alongside the other indent-aware regex factories:
```ts
blockquoteBeginRegex: (indent: number) => new RegExp(`^ {0,${Math.min(3, indent - 1)}}>`),
```

**`src/Tokenizer.ts`**: Declared and applied the regex in the list item continuation loop, exactly mirroring how `fencesBeginRegex`, `headingBeginRegex`, and `htmlBeginRegex` already work. When the next line is a blockquote with fewer leading spaces than the current list item's `indent`, the loop breaks and stops consuming lines for that item — letting the blockquote be handled at the appropriate nesting level.
